questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a natural hazard?
Describe the effects of landslides on the environment.
questions.answer: Landslides can cause soil erosion, damage to infrastructure, loss of biodiversity, and changes in the landscape.

questions.q: Explain how earthquakes can trigger tsunamis. (15 marks)
questions.answer: When an earthquake occurs beneath the ocean, it can displace the water above it, creating a tsunami. The displaced water travels at high speeds and can cause widespread damage to coastal areas.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Landslides are often triggered by ______________ in the environment.
T/F: Volcanic eruptions are a type of natural hazard.
What are the main causes of droughts in India? (8 marks)
questions.answer: Droughts in India are caused by a combination of factors, including low rainfall, inefficient water management, and human activities such as deforestation and over-extraction of groundwater.

questions.q: Explain the different types of droughts that occur in India. (12 marks)
questions.answer: India experiences three types of droughts: meteorological, hydrological, and agricultural droughts. Meteorological droughts occur when there is a deficiency in rainfall, hydrological droughts occur when there is a deficiency in water supply, and agricultural droughts occur when crops are affected by a lack of water.

questions.q: Which of the following is a consequence of a tsunami?
Describe the effects of earthquakes on human settlements. (10 marks)
questions.answer: Earthquakes can cause widespread damage to human settlements, including the collapse of buildings, roads, and other infrastructure. They can also trigger landslides and tsunamis, leading to loss of life and displacement of people.

questions.q: T/F: Natural hazards can be prevented completely.
